title: NOC 53110 – Photographers | GTR Immigration
description: From commercial advertising to scientific documentation, NOC 53110 (Photographers) play a pivotal role in Canada’s visual communications. As a TEER 3 occupation, photography is a highly accessible path for creative professionals looking to migrate. Canada’s diverse landscapes and thriving fashion and tech industries provide a wide canvas for international photographers to build a lucrative career.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category5 – Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sportTEER Level3Major Group53 – Occupations in art, culture and sportSub-Major Group531 – Art, culture and sport occupationsMinor Group5311 – PhotographersNOC Code53110

duties: Study requirements of a particular assignment and decide on type of camera and film/digital media.
Determine lighting and background and set up equipment.
Operate still cameras to take photographs of people, places, or objects.
Adapt existing photographic images and create new digitized images to be included in multimedia products.
Use specialized equipment such as scanners and image manipulation software.
employment_requirements: A bachelor's degree in visual arts with a specialization in photography OR specialized training in photography is required.
Creative and technical ability, demonstrated by a portfolio of work, is critical.
Knowledge of computerized photography and digital imaging software is mandatory.
job_outlook: The outlook is Moderate. Growth in digital media and advertising ensures a steady flow of assignments, although competition in the freelance market remains high.
labour_market_demand: The rise of e-commerce has significantly boosted the demand for commercial and product photographers. Additionally, specialized areas like medical and forensic photography are seeing increased institutional hiring. Major cities like Toronto and Montreal remain the primary hubs for fashion and editorial work.
Self-Employment Opportunity: A high percentage of photographers operate successful independent businesses.
Technological Edge: Canada is a leader in digital imaging and post-production software.
Global Exposure: Opportunities to work with international brands headquartered in Canada.
pathways: Self-Employed Persons Program: Specifically designed for professional photographers with a proven track record.
Express Entry (FSW/CEC): Valid for photographers with institutional or corporate employment history.
Atlantic Immigration Program (AIP): For photographers with job offers in the Atlantic provinces.
